biological_process.description: homeostasis of number of cells within a tissue
regulation of cell growth involved in cardiac muscle cell development

molecular_function.description: extracellular matrix structural constituent conferring tensile strength

function_descriptions: Plays an adhesive role by integrating collagen bundles. It is probably associated with the surface of interstitial collagen fibrils via COL1. The COL2 domain may then serve as a rigid arm which sticks out from the fibril and protrudes the large N-terminal globular domain into the extracellular space, where it might interact with other matrix molecules or cell surface receptors (By similarity). {ECO:0000250, ECO:0000269|PubMed:2187872}.

pathways.pathway: Assembly of collagen fibrils and other multimeric structures
